mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously as long as the rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42% as of 2023-08-15 suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2023-08-15 remains relatively low
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening aggregate demand. This dual deterioration in consumer and residential investment channels produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492008M and HOUST at 1326K as of 2023-07-15
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month moving average signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism operates continuously when the condition is met.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 as of 2023-07-15 indicates expansion relative to 2017 baseline
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when INDPRO is above its 12-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month, signaling a weakening labor market inflection point. This threshold crossing triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk and forward earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100K jobs
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, the central bank faces a credibility constraint that limits its ability to tighten further without triggering financial stress. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate policy error.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.62 and FEDFUNDS at 1.42% as of 2023-08-15
regime_hint: constrained monetary policy regime with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 51% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This threshold crossing produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as terminal growth expectations are revised downward and multiple compression accelerates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both contract month-over-month simultaneously, signaling a synchronized supply and labor shock that produces immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability and earnings revisions cascade.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 and PAYEMS at 148177K as of 2023-07-15
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 1-year rolling window.
